'''Dwayne West''' (born 8 June 1980) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s. He played in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Hull FC]] as a {{rlp|wg}}er. He is the son of former Wigan player and coach [[Graeme West]].<ref name="telegraph" />

==Playing career==
West started his career at Wigan, débuting in 1999 and making three appearances before moving to St Helens in June 2000.<ref name="telegraph">{{cite news 
|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/4749161/St-Helens-fend-off-Leigh-to-capture-West.html
|title=St Helens fend off Leigh to capture West
|publisher=[[The Daily Telegraph]] 
|date=15 June 2000|accessdate=14 August 2009
| location=London
| first=John
| last=Whalley}}</ref> 
He remained at St. Helens for two years before joining [[Hull FC]] in 2003.<ref>{{cite news 
|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2816165.stm
|title=St Helens
|publisher=[[BBC]] 
|date=7 March 2003|access-date=14 August 2009}}</ref> 
However, on his début for Hull F.C. he sustained a serious knee injury and was subsequently forced to retire from the sport.<ref>{{cite news 
|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/knee-operation-leaves-west-facing-another-season-on-the-sidelines-598854.html
|title=Knee operation leaves West facing another season on the sidelines
|work=[[The Independent]] 
|date=26 February 2003|accessdate=14 August 2009
| location=London
| first=Dave
| last=Hadfield}}{{dead link|date=August 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

==="Wide to West"===
He is most remembered for his time at St Helens for his role in arguably the most famous try in Super League history - [[Chris Joynt]]'s try in the Qualifying Playoff at [[Knowsley Road]] vs. [[Bradford Bulls]] on 22 September 2000. With the Bulls leading 11-10, the clock was up and there was one last play for St Helens to score; if the ball went dead the game would finish. After the ball had been passed back and forth on the right hand side by the St Helens players, there was no way through. [[Sean Long]] switched the play left where he found West, who ran forward and beat two Bulls players before passing the ball inside to Joynt to score a dramatic try at the death.<ref>{{cite web 
|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VjhE7DOvD-c
 |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211221/VjhE7DOvD-c |archive-date=2021-12-21 |url-status=live|title=wide to west
|publisher=[[YouTube]] 
|date=30 May 2006|accessdate=14 August 2009}}{{cbignore}}</ref> 
The try soon became known as the 'Wide to West' try,<ref>{{cite web |title=Early passage denied |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5260131.early-passage-denied/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=17 March 2025 |date=10 October 2002}}</ref> named after the memorable commentary by [[Eddie Hemmings (rugby league)|Eddie Hemmings]]:

{{cquote|[[Paul Sculthorpe|Sculthorpe]] wants to get on with it, Bradford counting down, this is the last play! Long, kicks it wide, to [[Kevin Iro|Iro]]. Iro… to [[Steve Hall (rugby league)|Hall]]. Hall is trapped. Back it goes… to [[Sean Hoppe|Hoppe]], over the shoulder to Hall. There is [[Tim Jonkers|Jonkers]], here is Long. And Long fancies it, Long fancies it! It's wide to West, it's wide to West! Dwayne West! Inside to Joynt! Joynt, Joynt, Joooynt! Oh! Oh! Fantastic! They've won it, they've won it!
|30px|30px|Commentator Eddie Hemmings|[[Sky Sports]]}}
